415	
416	Advance `PROGRESS.md` when all current-stage objectives are met:
417	
418	- `autoformalize` → `prover` (all statements formalized)
419	- `prover` → `polish` (all sorries filled and verified)
420	- `polish` → `COMPLETE` (proofs clean, compile)

3078	  level: the sectionwise-linearity datum promotes the ab-group morphism to a genuine
3079	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Its \(\mathtt{@[simp]}\) companion
3080	  \(\mathtt{toPresheaf\_map\_homMk}\) records that forgetting back recovers \(g\), i.e.\
3081	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,X).\mathtt{map}\,(\mathtt{homMk}\,g\,hg) = g\). This is step
3082	  (ii) of the A-bridge \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}: once the
3083	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism has been glued, \(\mathtt{homMk}\) re-attaches the
3084	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linearity to produce the global morphism of modules.
3085	\end{definition}
3086	
3087	\begin{lemma}
3088	[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
3089	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
3090	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
3091	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
3092	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
3093	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
3094	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
3095	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
3096	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
3097	\end{lemma}
3098	
3099	\begin{proof}
3100	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_homMk, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
3101	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
3102	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
3103	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
3104	
3105	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
3106	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups. The morphisms-between-them presheaf
3107	  \(\mathcal{H} := \mathtt{presheafHom}\,(M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\,
3108	  (N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\), whose value at an open \(W\) is the abelian
3109	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
3110	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
3111	  \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\), which consumes the sheaf condition of \(N\).
3112	  Regarding \(\mathcal{H}\) as a \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\), its universal gluing
3113	  property \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) amalgamates any compatible family of local
3114	  sections of \(\mathcal{H}\) into a unique global section. This route is preferred over
3115	  the raw sieve-level amalgamation \(\mathtt{presheafHom\_isSheafFor}\), which carries
3116	  strictly more bookkeeping.
3117	
3118	  The load-bearing datum is, for each \(i\), the \emph{local section}
3119	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i \in \mathcal{H}.\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,U_i)\)
3120	  manufactured from \(f_i\). Its component at a pair \((V, h : V \to U_i)\) is the given
3121	  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications
3122	  that arise from the open-set equality \(\iota_i\bigl(\iota_i^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\) valid
3123	  for \(V \subseteq U_i\) (the image under the open immersion \(\iota_i : U_i \to X\) of
3124	  the preimage of \(V\) is \(V\) again). The genuine coherence risk of the whole
3125	  construction is concentrated in the \emph{naturality field} of
3126	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\): the requirement that these
3127	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugated components of \(f_i\) commute across the morphisms of
3128	  the slice category \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\). This is the one sub-piece that should be
3129	  built and verified first, as a standalone axiom-clean lemma.
3130	
3131	  This \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality is precisely the \emph{section-direction
3132	  slice} of the comparison packaged in \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}:
3133	  converting the local datum \(f_i\), which lives over the open-immersion pullback
3134	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U_i}\), into a section of \(\mathcal{H}\) indexed over the
3135	  slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\) is the one-sided realisation of the open-immersion
3136	  \(\leftrightarrow\) slice transport. As there, the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation is
3137	  controlled by the down-set identity \(\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}(V)) = V\) for
3138	  \(V \subseteq U_i\), and the coherence is automatic on the thin poset
3139	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). The dual restriction-iso
3140	  (\cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}) consumes the \emph{functor-direction} face of the
3141	  same equivalence; that the two share \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} as a
3142	  common root is why the shared bridge, once built, unblocks both at once.
3143	
3144	  The remaining four sub-steps are mechanical: (a) the
3145	  cocycle/\(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) condition on the family
3146	  \((\mathtt{localSection}\,i)_i\), which is exactly the assumed agreement of \(f_i\)
3147	  and \(f_j\) on the overlaps \(U_i \cap U_j\); (b) gluing via
3148	  \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) and converting the amalgamated section over
3149	  \(\mathrm{op}\,\top\) into a genuine morphism \(g \colon M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf}
3150	  \to N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf}\) of the underlying presheaves --- the
3151	  \(\top\)-section-to-morphism passage being the global-sections equivalence
3152	  \(\mathtt{presheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) of the
3153	  hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) (which, since \(\top\) is terminal in \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\),
3154	  lands the glued section as an honest morphism of presheaves), \emph{not} a hand-unfold
3155	  of the \(\top\)-section; (c) the
3156	  sectionwise linearity check; and (d) the recovery that the restriction of \(g\) to
3157	  each \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).
3158	
3159	  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
3160	  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
3161	  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
3162	  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the
3163	  ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this
3164	  global linearity through the scheme-level wrapper \cref{def:scheme_modules_homMk}
3165	  (\(\mathtt{homMk}\)) yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
3166	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); its restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its
3167	  uniqueness are both inherited from the ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).
3168	
3169	  This gluing engine is substantially larger than a routine glue: it is dominated by the
3170	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality field and the convert-and-recover bookkeeping, and
3171	  the naturality is the section-direction slice of
3172	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} --- so its true cost is bounded below by
3173	  building that shared bridge, not by the surrounding assembly. It is therefore to be
3174	  scoped as a multi-piece build --- with \(\mathtt{localSection}\), together with its
3175	  naturality, built as a standalone axiom-clean lemma first --- rather than a one-shot.
3176	  The step computes no tensor stalk --- it is purely a statement about gluing morphisms
3177	  of sheaves --- and so it does not re-enter the abandoned tensor-stalk commutation.
3178	\end{proof}
3179	
3180	\begin{remark}
3181	[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
3182	  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}
3183	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial,
3184	        lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat, lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict,
3185	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
3186	  Once \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,def:presheaf_dual,%
3187	  lem:internal_hom_isSheaf,lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} are available,
3188	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is discharged with no further missing object:
3189	  take \(L^{-1} := \mathtt{dual}\,L\) (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}), which is a line
3190	  bundle by \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}. The global contraction \(\varepsilon_L
3191	  \colon L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{not} obtained by sheafifying
3192	  the presheaf-level evaluation; it is instead built by \emph{gluing the canonical local

3195	  \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\), and the
3196	  canonical local contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\)
3197	  of the free rank-one module with its dual is the left unitor
3198	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, an isomorphism. These local contractions are canonical,
3199	  hence agree on overlaps, and so glue --- by the A-bridge
